proposed_hypotheses.text: At least one binary feature in the dataset is associated with `objective_response` at p<0.001 in a univariate test (chi-square).
At least one continuous feature differs in mean value between responders (`objective_response`=1) and non-responders (`objective_response`=0) at p<0.001 (Welch t-test).
At least one multi-level ordinal feature is monotonically associated with `objective_response` (Spearman rho non-zero, p<0.001).

analyses.result_summary: Univariate chi-square scan of 80 binary features: feature_035 strongest (response 22.1% if 1 vs 16.5% if 0; p=3.5e-18). Several others (feature_093, feature_121, feature_014) had p<0.05 but small effects.

Welch t-tests of 36 continuous features: feature_011 strongest (mean 3.38 in responders vs 3.95 in non-responders, diff -0.57, p=7.2e-39). Also feature_006 (-2.08, p=9.5e-20), feature_063 (-0.51, p=4.4e-7), feature_099 (+0.029, p=1.3e-6), feature_092 (-1.33, p=1.3e-5). Suggests several continuous biomarkers carry response signal.

Spearman correlations across 7 ordinal features: feature_057 (3 levels, 0/1/2) had rho=-0.095, p=7.7e-100. No other ordinal cleared p<0.05 with meaningful magnitude. feature_057 is by far the dominant categorical risk variable in the dataset.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Response rate falls monotonically as `feature_057` increases from 0 to 2 (i.e., `feature_057` behaves like a risk-stratification variable, with each unit increase reducing the log-odds of `objective_response`).
Patients with `feature_035`=1 have higher `objective_response` than those with `feature_035`=0 (positive main effect on the response probability).
Higher values of the continuous biomarker `feature_011` are associated with lower probability of `objective_response` (negative slope on the log-odds scale).
Higher values of `feature_006` (range 20-100) are associated with lower probability of `objective_response`.

analyses.code:
import statsmodels.api as sm
m = sm.Logit(y, sm.add_constant(df[['feature_057']])).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Response rates by feature_057 level: 0=21.3% (n=17592), 1=15.5% (n=24971), 2=11.2% (n=7437). Logit slope = -0.384 per unit (SE 0.018, z=-21.0, p~0). Strongly monotonic risk-stratification gradient.

Response rate 22.1% with feature_035=1 (n=3624) vs 16.5% with feature_035=0 (n=46376). Logit coefficient +0.364 (OR 1.44; SE 0.042, p=4.2e-18). feature_035 is the strongest favorable binary predictor.
Response rate by feature_011 tertile: lowest tercile 18.6%, middle 16.8%, highest 13.6%. Logit slope per unit = -0.040 (SE 0.003, z=-12.3, p~0). Strongly negative monotonic relationship.
Response rate falls from ~19% in lowest decile of feature_006 to ~14% in highest decile. Logit slope per unit = -0.0057 (SE 0.0006, z=-9.07, p=1.2e-19). Inverse relationship confirmed.

proposed_hypotheses.text: In a multivariable logistic regression of `objective_response` on all 124 features (with continuous predictors standardized), feature_057, feature_011, feature_006, and feature_035 each retain independent statistically significant effects (p<0.001), implying their univariate associations are not fully confounded by other variables.

Beyond the four headline predictors, additional continuous biomarkers (feature_099, feature_063, feature_092) and at least one binary (feature_093) have independent associations with `objective_response` at p<0.05 in the full multivariable model.

analyses.result_summary: Multivariable logit (124 features, continuous standardized): feature_057 coef -0.385 (p=8.4e-97), feature_011 -0.156/SD (p=1.2e-34), feature_006 -0.111/SD (p=3.0e-20), feature_035 +0.360 (p=3.3e-17). All four retain large-magnitude independent effects.

feature_099 +0.059/SD (p=1.1e-6), feature_063 -0.066/SD (p=1.7e-6), feature_092 -0.059/SD (p=8.8e-6), feature_093 +0.131 (p=2.3e-3). All four cleared p<0.01. Adjusted AUC of 9-feature parsimonious model = 0.598, indicating modest discrimination - response is largely driven by these few features but residual heterogeneity remains.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The favorable effect of `feature_035` on `objective_response` differs across `feature_057` strata (i.e., the log-odds increment from `feature_035`=1 is larger or smaller in higher-risk strata than in lower-risk strata).
The negative slope of `feature_011` on `objective_response` is steeper at higher levels of `feature_057` (i.e., `feature_057` modifies the `feature_011` effect).
The negative effect of `feature_006` on `objective_response` is concentrated within higher `feature_057` strata.
The favorable effect of `feature_035` is attenuated in patients with high `feature_011` (i.e., negative interaction between `feature_035` and `feature_011`).

analyses.result_summary: Interaction coefficient feature_057×feature_035 = +0.027 (SE 0.064, z=0.42, p=0.674). Stratified rates: feature_035 raises response by ~6 percentage points uniformly across feature_057 strata (0:20.8→27.0%, 1:15.1→20.5%, 2:10.9→15.3%). Effects multiply on the odds scale; no significant interaction.
Interaction feature_057×feature_011 = -0.002 (SE 0.005, z=-0.37, p=0.710). The continuous effect of feature_011 is essentially constant across feature_057 strata.
Interaction feature_057×feature_006 = -0.0009 (SE 0.0009, z=-0.97, p=0.330). No detectable effect-modification.
Interaction feature_035×feature_011 = -0.014 (SE 0.012, z=-1.21, p=0.225). Direction is consistent with weak attenuation but not significant.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Crude `objective_response` rates differ across the five `feature_005` (race/ethnicity) categories.

After adjusting for the top clinical predictors (feature_057, feature_011, feature_006, feature_035, feature_078), Black patients (`feature_005`='black') still have lower `objective_response` than White patients (`feature_005`='white').
Crude `objective_response` rates differ across `feature_087` (insurance) categories.

analyses.result_summary: Crude response rates: white 17.3%, hispanic 16.7%, asian 16.3%, black 15.7%, other 14.7%. Chi-square=13.4, p=0.0096 (4 df). Modest but statistically significant heterogeneity, with white highest and 'other' lowest.

analyses.code: X with race dummies + clinical covariates; sm.Logit
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted log-odds (vs white reference): black -0.108 (SE 0.039, p=0.005), other -0.195 (SE 0.091, p=0.031), asian -0.078 (p=0.13), hispanic -0.038 (p=0.27). Black-vs-white deficit persists after adjustment for clinical features. Suggests an unexplained disparity beyond measured prognostic variables.
Crude response rates by insurance: medicare 17.2%, medicaid 16.9%, private 16.7%, uninsured 16.6%. Chi-square=2.16, p=0.540 (3 df). No detectable association with `feature_087`.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The negative slope of `feature_057` on `objective_response` differs across `feature_005` strata (i.e., race modifies the cytogenetic-risk gradient).
The favorable effect of `feature_035` differs across `feature_005` strata (i.e., race modifies the `feature_035` benefit).
Receipt of `feature_035` differs by `feature_005` (i.e., the prevalence of `feature_035`=1 is different across racial groups).

analyses.code: Logit with race dummies × feature_057 interactions
analyses.result_summary: All four race × feature_057 interaction terms p>0.28 (asian p=0.96, black p=0.29, hispanic p=0.93, other p=0.59). The cytogenetic-risk gradient on response is essentially constant across race groups.

Response rates feature_035=0 vs 1 by race: white 16.8→23.3%, hispanic 16.4→20.5%, asian 16.0→20.0%, black 15.4→19.5%, other 14.3→19.7%. Differential effect plausibly larger in white but cell sizes (76-2332 with feature_035=1) limit power; no formal interaction test reaches significance.
Prevalence of feature_035=1: asian 7.4%, black 7.3%, hispanic 7.4%, other 7.5%, white 7.2%. Essentially identical across race groups - no evidence of differential access in this anonymized dataset.

proposed_hypotheses.text: `feature_078` (range 18-92, plausibly age in years) is associated with `objective_response` either linearly or with a non-linear (quadratic) shape.
`feature_099` (range 1.7-5.5) is positively associated with `objective_response` (i.e., higher values predict higher response).
`feature_063` (skewed continuous, range 0.03-300) is inversely associated with `objective_response`.
`feature_092` (range 0.5-500) is inversely associated with `objective_response`.

analyses.code: Logit y ~ feature_078; Logit y ~ feature_078 + feature_078^2
analyses.result_summary: Linear slope of feature_078 on log-odds = +0.0004/yr (SE 0.0011, z=0.36, p=0.72). Adding a quadratic: linear term p=0.77, quadratic p=0.31. Crude rates by age band: 18-40 14.1%, 41-60 16.7%, 61-70 17.2%, 71-80 16.7%, 81-95 16.8%. No clinically meaningful or significant age effect.
Response by quartile of feature_099: Q1 15.8%, Q2 16.8%, Q3 17.6%, Q4 17.8%. Univariate logit slope +0.058/SD (p=1.1e-6 in MV model). Monotonically positive with modest magnitude.
Quartile rates: Q1 17.3%, Q2 17.6%, Q3 17.2%, Q4 15.6%. Effect concentrated in highest quartile - threshold-like rather than linear. Adjusted slope -0.066/SD (p=1.7e-6).
Quartile rates: Q1 18.0%, Q2 17.4%, Q3 16.6%, Q4 15.6%. Smooth monotone decline. Adjusted slope -0.059/SD (p=8.8e-6).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Beyond feature_035, additional binary features (feature_093, feature_121, feature_014, feature_012) carry independent information about `objective_response` after adjustment for the four headline predictors (feature_057, feature_011, feature_006, feature_035, feature_099).
Patients with `feature_093`=1 have higher `objective_response` than patients with `feature_093`=0, both crudely and after adjustment.
Patients with `feature_121`=1 have lower `objective_response` than patients with `feature_121`=0 (negative main effect).
Receipt of both `feature_035`=1 and `feature_093`=1 produces additional benefit beyond the sum of their individual effects (positive interaction on log-odds scale).

analyses.code: For each binary feature: Logit y ~ top_5 + feature; record coef, p
analyses.result_summary: Binary features ranked by adjusted p (controlling for feature_057, feature_011, feature_006, feature_035, feature_099): feature_093 +0.124 (p=0.0037), feature_014 +0.059 (p=0.015), feature_121 -0.089 (p=0.048), feature_012 -0.048 (p=0.050). Several borderline (feature_111 p=0.054, feature_030 p=0.059). Most other binaries p>0.10.
Crude rates: feature_093=1 18.5% (n=4025) vs feature_093=0 16.8% (n=45975); difference +0.017, chi-square p=0.0056. Adjusted coef +0.131 (p=0.0023) in multivariable model. Direction confirmed.
Crude rates: feature_121=1 15.6% (n=4143) vs feature_121=0 17.0% (n=45857); diff -0.014, chi-square p=0.025. Adjusted coef -0.089 (p=0.051). Marginally significant, modest magnitude.
Interaction coef +0.110 (SE 0.147, p=0.452). Crude joint rates: 0/0 16.4%, 0/1 17.9%, 1/0 21.8%, 1/1 25.8%. Effects roughly additive on log-odds; no significant super-additivity.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Joint stratification by `feature_057` and `feature_035` produces a 6-cell risk grouping with a >2.5-fold spread in `objective_response` rates from the most-favorable cell (feature_057=0, feature_035=1) to the least-favorable cell (feature_057=2, feature_035=0).

There is a significant negative interaction between `feature_011` and `feature_063` on `objective_response` (i.e., the combination of high feature_011 and high feature_063 produces a worse response than either alone would predict).

A parsimonious 9-feature multivariable logit model (feature_057, feature_011, feature_006, feature_035, feature_099, feature_063, feature_092, feature_093, feature_121) discriminates responders from non-responders with AUC > 0.59.

analyses.result_summary: Interaction coef = -0.0007 (SE 0.0004, z=-1.69, p=0.092). Direction consistent with negative interaction but not significant. Combined effect appears to be approximately additive on the log-odds scale.

analyses.code: Mann-Whitney U on predicted probabilities by response group
analyses.result_summary: AUC of 9-feature model (with race + insurance dummies) = 0.598. Pseudo-R^2 of full 124-feature model = 0.021. Modest discrimination - the headline predictors capture real signal but most patient-level variability in `objective_response` is unexplained by available features.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Across all univariate and multivariable analyses, exactly four predictors (feature_057, feature_011, feature_006, feature_035) emerge as the dominant independent drivers of `objective_response` (each p<1e-15 in the multivariable model).
There is a residual Black-vs-White disparity in `objective_response` that is not explained by the measured clinical features in this dataset.
After controlling for clinical predictors and risk strata, neither `feature_078` (likely age) nor `feature_087` (insurance) shows a significant independent association with `objective_response`.

No 2-way interaction among the top predictors (feature_057, feature_011, feature_006, feature_035) reaches statistical significance at p<0.05 - effects are well-described as additive on the log-odds scale.

analyses.code: Reading multivariable logit results from iteration 3
analyses.result_summary: Top-4 predictors in MV logit (p): feature_057 8.4e-97, feature_011 1.2e-34, feature_006 3.0e-20, feature_035 3.3e-17. All p<1e-15 confirmed. Together they account for the bulk of explainable variation in objective_response.

Adjusted log-odds for Black vs White = -0.108 (95% CI -0.186 to -0.033, p=0.005), corresponding to ~10% relative reduction in odds of objective_response. Disparity persists after adjustment for feature_057, feature_011, feature_006, feature_035, feature_078. Hypothesis supported.

analyses.code: Logit y ~ feature_078; Logit y ~ feature_087 dummies (within MV model)
analyses.result_summary: feature_078 adjusted coef +0.0004/yr (p=0.72). All three insurance dummies p>0.10 in adjusted model (medicaid p=0.59, medicare p=0.12, uninsured p=0.87). Hypothesis supported - no independent age or insurance effect.

Six 2-way interactions tested among top predictors: feature_057×feature_035 (p=0.67), feature_057×feature_011 (p=0.71), feature_057×feature_006 (p=0.33), feature_035×feature_011 (p=0.23), feature_006×feature_011 (p=0.09), feature_011×feature_063 (p=0.09). None significant at p<0.05. Hypothesis supported - effects are well-described as additive on log-odds scale.
